response.justification: The candidate is an experienced generalist with strong web, Node.js/TypeScript, and D-language backgrounds, but does not meet the core requirements of this role. C++ exposure is limited to a roughly six-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project — far short of the mandated 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. There is no SQL Server or Oracle experience listed (MongoDB/MySQL only), and no fixed-income or capital-markets domain depth; the closest match is ~15 months at a fintech (RIDE Capital) doing Node/TypeScript work. The gap on all mandatory criteria makes this a poor fit despite solid engineering fundamentals.
strengths: Broad, senior-level engineering experience across web, backend, and low-latency systems
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing, trunk-based development)
Some financial-services exposure via RIDE Capital investment platform work
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (~6 months in 2014–2015), not the required 5–10 years on Windows
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MongoDB/MySQL
No fixed income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain expertise

key_factors.factor: Solid general engineering and CI/CD fundamentals
